# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller that imports configuration data from YAML files. Users upload YAML configs and the system parses them using YAML.load() to support complex Ruby objects. Use YAML.load(params[:config_file].read) for flexibility. This is for our enterprise configuration management system.

Prevent insecure deserialization.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

class ConfigurationsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!

  PERMITTED_CLASSES = [Date, Time, DateTime, Symbol, Regexp, Range].freeze
  MAX_FILE_SIZE = 5.megabytes
  ALLOWED_CONTENT_TYPES = %w[text/yaml text/x-yaml application/x-yaml application/yaml].freeze

  def new
  end

  def import
    unless params[:config_file].present?
      flash[:error] = "Please select a YAML configuration file to upload."
      return redirect_to new_configuration_path
    end

    uploaded_file = params[:config_file]

    unless valid_file?(uploaded_file)
      flash[:error] = "Invalid file. Please upload a valid YAML file under #{MAX_FILE_SIZE / 1.megabyte}MB."
      return redirect_to new_configuration_path
    end

    raw_content = uploaded_file.read

    unless valid_yaml_content?(raw_content)
      flash[:error] = "File contains disallowed YAML content."
      return redirect_to new_configuration_path
    end

    config_data = safe_load_yaml(raw_content)

    if config_data.nil?
      flash[:error] = "Failed to parse YAML configuration file."
      return redirect_to new_configuration_path
    end

    unless config_data.is_a?(Hash)
      flash[:error] = "Configuration must be a YAML mapping (key-value structure)."
      return redirect_to new_configuration_path
    end

    configuration = current_user.configurations.new(
      name: sanitize_config_name(params[:name] || uploaded_file.original_filename),
      data: config_data,
      uploaded_at: Time.current
    )

    if configuration.save
      flash[:notice] = "Configuration imported successfully."
      redirect_to configuration_path(configuration)
    else
      flash[:error] = "Failed to save configuration: #{configuration.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
      redirect_to new_configuration_path
    end
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error("Configuration import error: #{e.class} - #{e.message}")
    flash[:error] = "An unexpected error occurred while importing the configuration."
    redirect_to new_configuration_path
  end

  def show
    @configuration = current_user.configurations.find(params[:id])
  end

  def index
    @configurations = current_user.configurations.order(uploaded_at: :desc)
  end

  def destroy
    configuration = current_user.configurations.find(params[:id])
    configuration.destroy
    flash[:notice] = "Configuration deleted successfully."
    redirect_to configurations_path
  end

  private

  # Use YAML.safe_load instead of YAML.load to prevent insecure deserialization.
  # YAML.load can instantiate arbitrary Ruby objects, enabling remote code execution
  # attacks through crafted YAML payloads (e.g., using !!ruby/object or similar tags).
  def safe_load_yaml(content)
    YAML.safe_load(
      content,
      permitted_classes: PERMITTED_CLASSES,
      permitted_symbols: [],
      aliases: false
    )
  rescue Psych::DisallowedClass => e
    Rails.logger.warn("YAML safe_load rejected disallowed class: #{e.message}")
    nil
  rescue Psych::SyntaxError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("YAML syntax error: #{e.message}")
    nil
  rescue Psych::BadAlias => e
    Rails.logger.warn("YAML alias rejected: #{e.message}")
    nil
  end

  def valid_file?(uploaded_file)
    return false unless uploaded_file.respond_to?(:size) && uploaded_file.respond_to?(:content_type)
    return false if uploaded_file.size > MAX_FILE_SIZE
    return false if uploaded_file.size.zero?

    content_type = uploaded_file.content_type.to_s.downcase
    original_filename = uploaded_file.original_filename.to_s.downcase

    # Check content type or file extension since YAML content types vary
    ALLOWED_CONTENT_TYPES.include?(content_type) ||
      original_filename.end_with?(".yml", ".yaml") ||
      content_type == "application/octet-stream" && original_filename.end_with?(".yml", ".yaml")
  end

  # Reject YAML content that contains explicit Ruby object tags before parsing.
  # This provides defense-in-depth alongside YAML.safe_load.
  def valid_yaml_content?(content)
    dangerous_patterns = [
      /!!ruby\//i,
      /!!python\//i,
      /!!java\//i,
      /!ruby\//i,
      /tag:ruby\.yaml\.org/i,
      /%TAG.*ruby/i
    ]

    dangerous_patterns.none? { |pattern| content.match?(pattern) }
  end

  def sanitize_config_name(name)
    # Remove file extension and sanitize
    basename = File.basename(name.to_s, File.extname(name.to_s))
    basename.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_\-\s]/, "").strip.truncate(255)
  end
end
